message:
feat(agent-config): provider-aware root resolution, two-marker section validation, and concurrent apply safety

Agent config path resolution now respects provider-specific root directory
preferences (e.g. .cursor before .config/Cursor) and validates that a
managed section has both start AND end markers before treating it as
replaceable.

Root resolution and config path selection:
- Add preferred_root_suffixes() and root_precedence() to rank detected
  root directories by provider-canonical ordering
- Add preferred_root_path() public API for selecting the best root from
  a list of candidates
- Add detected_config_path() to find actual config files (not just root
  directories) in detected roots
- convert_detection_entry() now uses file-existence checks instead of
  blindly taking the first root path
- resolve_agent_config_root() in main.rs now prefers config_path over
  root_paths, then falls back to provider-preferred root order
- Test: root-only entries without config files are no longer counted as
  "configured" in AgentInventoryData

Section validation and concurrent safety:
- Extract section_is_present() requiring both SECTION_START_MARKER and
  SECTION_END_MARKER (unterminated blocks are now treated as append, not
  replace)
- Extract classify_agent_config_action() and
  agent_config_section_is_present() in main.rs for consistent logic
- apply_prepared_agent_config() now re-reads the target file at apply
  time so concurrent modifications between prepare and apply are merged
  correctly instead of being silently overwritten
- Action counting in the configure command now uses the result action
  string (reflecting actual filesystem outcome) rather than the planned
  action enum

Robot API reference updates in templates:
- panes list → state, panes inspect → get-text, panes send-text → send
- search --explain → search-explain, status → health alias
- sessions list removed in favor of state/health

Tests: config path preference with multiple roots, concurrent merge
safety, skip-when-current detection, malformed section handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
